highlights.title: Show 'Initiating' when a bolus starts
changes: Trio now shows an 'initiating' state on the bolus progress view when a bolus is started.
The bolus progress indicator displays the initiating state until insulin delivery begins.
The initiating state text is included in the app localizations.

highlights.title: Copy SHA and version info from Settings
changes: You can select and copy the SHA and submodule names in the Settings view.
A copy button is added to the version header to copy full app version, branch, and SHA to the clipboard.
Copying now shows a brief "Copied" confirmation toast and provides haptic feedback.
Version copy was changed from a long-press gesture to a normal button for easier use.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1214: Enable text selection to copy SHA

highlights.title: Live Activity glucose forecast on lock screen
changes: Adds a new Display Glucose Forecasts toggle under Settings → Notifications → Live Activity, defaulting to off.
When enabled, the Live Activity lock-screen chart shows Oref glucose forecasts as either a cone or lines.
The forecast display type follows the existing Forecast Display Type setting in the main app.
Chart scaling and rendering were adjusted so forecast values fit the small lock-screen chart and remain readable.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1194: Live Activity: Adding glucose forecast
feat: Add Display Glucose Forecasts toggle to Live Activity settings

highlights.title: Add Eversense CGM support
changes: Eversense is added as a selectable CGM option in the app.
Trio now includes support for Eversense CGM devices (E3 and 365).
The app embeds the EversenseKit framework so it can connect to Eversense sensors.
Optional upload to the Eversense DMS, including debug data, is available and can be opted out.

includedPullRequests.title: Feat: Bolus initiating
includedPullRequests.body:
(duplicate of #1206)
(provided feedback from previous PR https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1206#issuecomment-4702981172)

This PR updates the bolus progress view to show to the user that the bolus is initiating.
This is required for the upcomming TandemKit driver, because the Tandem Mobi takes ~5s between accepting a bolus command and actually progressing the bolus.

We could also add the last bolus state: canceling. But this is not done in this PR

Demo video:

<video src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2c22ff5c-d203-4305-bb1f-c64019df2192" />

includedPullRequests.body: resolves #1210 Allows users to easily copy the SHA of Trio or of a specific submodule.

includedPullRequests.title: Live Activity: Adding glucose forecast
includedPullRequests.body: New feature adding glucose forecast to the Live Activity lock screen widget. Quick walkthrough: - The feature is controlled by a setting under Settings - Notifications - Live Activity - Default setting is off - Forecast display type (cone or lines) is controlled by the same setting as the Main chart | Setting, default is off | Setting explanation | Example with prediction lines | Example with cone | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | Runs fine on my test device and on my live device, but needs more testers and a code review.

Quick walkthrough: - The feature is controlled by a setting under Settings - Notifications - Live Activity - Default setting is off - Forecast display type (cone or lines) is controlled by the same setting as the Main chart | Setting, default is off | Setting explanation | Example with prediction lines | Example with cone | |--------|--------|--------|--------| | | | | | Runs fine on my test device and on my live device, but needs more testers and a code review.

includedPullRequests.title: Feature: EversenseKit
includedPullRequests.body:
The PR will add the EversenseKit driver to Trio.
I still need to investigate a few crashes from Joanne, thus it stays on Draft for now.

The driver is compatible with the Eversense E3 (both 90 days and 180 days) and the Eversense 365.
It has support for uploading directly to the Eversense DMS including debug data for Eversense Support.
This is an opt-out feature
